key_insight: AutoDFT turns DFT from a one-shot scripted workflow into a closed-loop multi-agent system that plans, executes, monitors, and repairs calculations adaptively.
engineering_relevance: This could make first-principles materials simulations more reliable and accessible for non-experts by reducing manual intervention when convergence issues or unexpected physics arise.
method: Strategic planner creates a skeletal DFT task plan.
Step planner generates numerical parameters just in time from prior outputs.
Monitor-recover-reflect loop diagnoses failures, repairs them, and revises the plan when needed.
evidence_signal: In VASPBench, a benchmark of 34 tasks across 9 DFT calculation types, AutoDFT reached 94.1% task-level success with GPT-5.2 and also showed reliable property prediction on materials databases.
watch_item: Performance depends on robust LLM reasoning and recovery logic, so edge cases, tool errors, or poorly specified physics may still require expert oversight.
paper_title: AutoDFT: A Closed-Loop Multi-Agent Framework for Autonomous DFT Calculations
